result:
I’d make “truth and trustworthiness” the default incentive in public life—so institutions, media, and leaders are rewarded for being accurate, transparent, and corrigible, and penalized (quickly and reliably) for deception and reckless misinformation.

Because if you get that one lever right, a lot of other problems become tractable: better policy decisions, less polarization, faster scientific progress, more effective coordination on climate and pandemics, and fewer people exploited by scams or propaganda.

content.text:

Because if you get that one lever right, a lot of other problems become tractable: better policy decisions, less polarization, faster scientific progress, more effective coordination on climate and pandemics, and fewer people exploited by scams or propaganda.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
